draft.introduction: Surah Yunus (Jonah) addresses the themes of divine guidance, the transience of worldly life, and the stories of previous prophets including Noah, Moses, and Jonah. It emphasizes that the Quran is not fabricated but confirmed by divine signs.

translation.text: Alif. Lam. Ra. These are the verses of the wise Book. Is it a wonder to people that We revealed to a man from among them: ‘Warn mankind and give good tidings to the believers.’ Indeed, your Lord is God, who created the heavens and the earth in six days and then established Himself above the Throne, arranging all affairs.

translator_notes.note: God’s “establishment above the Throne” (istawa ‘ala al-‘arsh) became one of the most debated theological questions in Islam. The Ash’aris affirm it “without asking how” (bila kayf), while the Mu’tazila interpreted it metaphorically to preserve divine transcendence.
